title: deer-flow.cycle.wait.exit v1
description: Emitted by `deer cycle wait` when blocking on a stack-tuner cycle finishes — either because the cycle reached a TERMINAL_VERDICTS state (reason=terminal) or because the wait timed out (reason=timeout). PRODUCER NOTE: this channel is currently emitted by scripts/deer's `_emit_bus_event` helper which writes a FLAT dict (no CloudEvents envelope, no `data` block) — top-level keys are `type`, `ts`, `cycle_id`, `reason`, `elapsed_s`, and on terminal: `event`, `verdict`, `cost_usd`. This schema describes that flat shape as currently emitted; the canonical CloudEvents-wrapped shape is tracked for migration. Producer source: scripts/deer::_cycle_wait.

type.description: literal channel name (flat-envelope only — drop when migrated to CloudEvents wrapper)

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp emitted by the producer

cycle_id.description: stack-tuner cycle id this wait was blocking on

elapsed_s.description: wall-clock seconds spent blocking (rounded to 1 decimal)

verdict.description: terminal-only — the TERMINAL_VERDICTS value the cycle reached (e.g. ship, abort, needs_pro_tuning)

cost_usd.description: terminal-only — cumulative cycle cost in USD
